description: Comprehensive FULL-mode documentation of methodological details, limitations, and reproducibility for the SAE absorption cross-domain study. Seven methodology notes covering aggregation, position asymmetry, single-model limitation, probe quality confound (with H10 findings), reproducibility, activation patching correction, and decoder magnitude comparison.

methodology_notes.title: Per-Token Aggregation Method for Absorption Measurement
methodology_notes.description: Absorption rates are computed as the fraction of probe-correct tokens that become false negatives after SAE reconstruction, aggregated per-token rather than per-unique-word or per-unique-entity.

details.definition_of_false_negative: A token where: (1) the linear probe correctly predicts the parent class on raw model activations, BUT (2) the probe incorrectly predicts the parent class on SAE-reconstructed activations, AND (3) the main feature for that class fires (i.e., the SAE does encode the feature, but it gets absorbed by another latent).

first_letter_specifics.unit_of_analysis: Token occurrence (each word x prompt combination is one observation)
first_letter_specifics.note: 500 unique words x 3 prompts = 1500 total tokens. Of these, 1138 had correct probe predictions on raw activations. 308 of those became false negatives after SAE reconstruction.

cross_domain_specifics.unit_of_analysis: Entity (each city in the RAVEL dataset is one observation)
cross_domain_specifics.note: Each city entity appears once. Probe is evaluated on the entity's activation at position -2. No multi-prompt averaging for RAVEL hierarchies.

per_unique_word_mean.description: Average each word's absorption rate across its prompts, then average over words. Prevents high-frequency words from dominating.
per_unique_word_mean.note: Higher than per-token (0.2707) because words with fewer correct predictions can have extreme rates.

per_letter_mean.description: Average each letter's absorption rate, then average over letters. Each letter contributes equally regardless of frequency.
per_letter_mean.note: Lower than per-token because rare letters (j, q, z) with 0% absorption pull down the mean.

details.justification: We report the per-token rate (FN / probe_correct) as the primary metric because: (1) it is the natural estimator of the probability that a correctly-probed token loses its feature after SAE reconstruction, (2) it matches the sae_spelling convention, (3) bootstrap confidence intervals are computed on this quantity. We also report per-word and per-letter rates for robustness; the qualitative finding (significant cross-domain variation) holds under all aggregation methods.

methodology_notes.title: Token Position Asymmetry Between First-Letter and RAVEL Hierarchies
methodology_notes.description: First-letter absorption uses token position -6, while RAVEL hierarchies use position -2. This reflects different data generation conventions but may affect comparability.

first_letter_position.explanation: The sae_spelling library positions the target word 6 tokens from the end of the prompt. Probes are trained and evaluated at this position. The prompt structure is: 'The word [WORD] begins with the letter' followed by padding tokens, placing the word at position -6 relative to sequence end.
first_letter_position.note: All 4 layers (L6, L12, L18, L24) achieve perfect F1=1.0 at position -6, indicating the model robustly represents first-letter information at this position.

ravel_position.explanation: RAVEL prompts place the entity (city name) at the second-to-last position. The prompt structure from RAVEL templates is: 'The city of [ENTITY] is located in' with the entity occupying the -2 position. Probes are trained and evaluated at this position.

impact_assessment.concern: Different token positions may affect the model's internal representation quality, potentially confounding cross-domain absorption comparisons.
impact_assessment.mitigation_1: Both positions are chosen to maximize probe quality for their respective tasks. Using position -2 for first-letter would degrade probe quality; using position -6 for RAVEL would require rewriting prompts.
impact_assessment.mitigation_2: The absorption measurement is always relative to the same probe that was trained at that position. The question 'does the SAE lose information that the raw model has?' is valid regardless of absolute position.
impact_assessment.mitigation_3: The primary cross-domain comparison (H1: Kruskal-Wallis test) compares the three RAVEL hierarchies that all use position -2 against each other, so position asymmetry does not affect the within-RAVEL comparison. The 4x range finding (11.6%-45.1%) is entirely within-RAVEL.
impact_assessment.remaining_caveat: First-letter (27.1%) vs RAVEL comparisons should be interpreted with caution given the position difference. However, the H10 probe degradation ablation (iter_010 Phase 1) will control for this by degrading first-letter probes at the same position (-6) to match RAVEL F1 levels.
impact_assessment.h10_preliminary_finding: H10 probe degradation: degrading F1=1.0 to ~0.80 increased absorption from 0.269 to 0.356.

methodology_notes.title: Single-Model Limitation (Gemma 2 2B Only)
methodology_notes.description: All experiments use a single model (Gemma 2 2B). No cross-model validation has been performed. This limits generalizability claims.

why_single_model.reason_1: Gemma Scope provides the largest publicly available collection of pre-trained SAEs for a single model, enabling systematic comparison across SAE architectures (JumpReLU, BatchTopK, Matryoshka) and widths (16k, 65k).
why_single_model.reason_2: The sae_spelling library was developed and validated on Gemma 2 2B, providing a reliable positive control for first-letter absorption.
why_single_model.reason_3: RAVEL probes have been validated on Gemma 2 2B in prior work, ensuring probe quality is not a methodological bottleneck.
why_single_model.reason_4: Computational budget: each iteration requires ~3.5 GPU-hours. Testing N models would multiply this by N.

generalizability_concerns: Absorption rates may differ in larger models (e.g., 7B, 13B, 70B) where SAEs have different sparsity-capacity tradeoffs.
Different model families (GPT-2, Pythia, Llama, Qwen) may exhibit different absorption patterns due to architectural differences (attention patterns, layer normalization, residual stream structure).
The hierarchy-specific pattern (city-country > first-letter > city-continent > city-language) may not generalize to other models if it depends on Gemma 2's specific training distribution.
The architecture invariance finding (hierarchy >> architecture) may not hold for models with very different residual stream dimensions.
future_work_recommendations: Test on GPT-2 (small/medium/large/XL) using existing SAE Bench SAEs to validate cross-model consistency.
Test on Pythia suite (70M to 12B) to examine scaling behavior of absorption.
Test on Llama 3 if SAEs become publicly available.
Prioritize models where both pre-trained SAEs and validated probes exist.

methodology_notes.title: Probe Quality Confound: F1 Correlation with Absorption Rates
methodology_notes.description: There is a strong negative correlation between probe F1 and measured absorption rates across hierarchies (rho=-0.756). This raises the concern that apparent cross-domain variation may be an artifact of probe quality differences rather than genuine hierarchy-dependent absorption.

observed_correlation.note: Negative correlation: lower probe F1 correlates with higher measured absorption. However, city-language (F1=0.818) has the LOWEST absorption (11.6%), breaking the simple monotonic pattern.

causal_mechanism_for_confound.explanation: If the probe is imperfect (F1 < 1.0), some tokens that the probe classifies as 'correct on raw activations' may actually be marginal or lucky. After SAE reconstruction introduces noise, these marginal correct predictions become incorrect, inflating the false negative count. This would artificially increase measured absorption.
causal_mechanism_for_confound.implication: Cross-domain variation in absorption could be partially or fully explained by cross-domain variation in probe quality.

counter_evidence.city_language_anomaly: City-language has intermediate F1 (0.818) but the LOWEST absorption (11.6%). If probe quality were the sole driver, city-language should have intermediate absorption. This suggests a genuine hierarchy effect.
counter_evidence.within_hierarchy_consistency: Within first-letter, absorption rates are consistent across probes that all have F1=1.0 (layers 6, 12, 18, 24). The variation across layers (0.7% at L6 to 27.1% at L24) tracks SAE reconstruction quality, not probe quality.
counter_evidence.kruskal_wallis_within_ravel: Even comparing only the three RAVEL hierarchies (all with imperfect probes), there is massive variation: 11.6% to 45.1%, Kruskal-Wallis p=7.37e-66.

resolution_plan.h10_probe_degradation_ablation: Iter_010 Phase 1 (HIGHEST PRIORITY experiment) directly addresses this confound by artificially degrading first-letter probes from F1=1.0 to F1={0.70, 0.80, 0.85, 0.90} and re-measuring absorption. If degraded first-letter rates match RAVEL rates at equivalent F1 levels, the confound is confirmed. If a gap persists, the hierarchy effect is genuine.
resolution_plan.interpretation_if_artifact: Important methodological caution for the field: absorption measurements are probe-quality-dependent. Future work must control for probe quality when comparing across domains.
resolution_plan.interpretation_if_genuine: Cross-domain absorption variation is a real phenomenon, robust to probe quality differences. The confound concern is empirically dismissed.

h10_probe_degradation_results.verdict_explanation: At F1~0.80, first-letter absorption (0.3559) falls within RAVEL range [0.1156, 0.4510]. Variation is primarily a probe quality artifact.

methodology_notes.title: Reproducibility Details: Seeds, Library Versions, SAE Identifiers, and Dataset Specifications
methodology_notes.description: Complete specification of all random seeds, software versions, SAE model identifiers, and dataset configurations needed to reproduce the experiments.

random_seeds.usage: Used for all random operations: probe train/test splits, bootstrap resampling, permutation tests, entity sampling, noise injection in H10 probe degradation.

hardware.gpu: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition

first_letter.prompt_template: The word {WORD} begins with the letter

city_country.note: Original dataset has 80 classes; 3 excluded due to <5 entities

ravel.prompt_source: RAVEL city_prompt templates (Attribute-specific templates from the dataset)

first_letter_probes.input: Raw model residual stream activations at specified layer and position

ravel_probes.note: Probes trained on all RAVEL city entities with their prompt templates. F1 reported as weighted macro F1.

kruskal_wallis.note: Non-parametric test chosen because absorption rates are binary (0/1 per token) and distributions are non-normal.

wilcoxon_signed_rank.usage: Paired comparison of recovery rates (child-zeroed vs control) in activation patching.

cohens_d.usage: Effect size for activation patching and other paired comparisons.

absorption_measurement_algorithm.step_1: Load model and SAE. Encode input tokens through model to get residual stream activations at target layer.
absorption_measurement_algorithm.step_2: Apply linear probe to raw activations. Record prediction. If incorrect, skip this token (not probe-correct).
absorption_measurement_algorithm.step_3: Pass raw activations through SAE encoder to get latent activations z = ReLU(W_enc * x + b_enc).
absorption_measurement_algorithm.step_5: Apply same linear probe to reconstructed activations x_hat. Record prediction.
absorption_measurement_algorithm.step_6: If step 2 correct but step 5 incorrect: this is a false negative (potential absorption).
absorption_measurement_algorithm.step_7: Check if the main feature for the true class fires (z[main_feature_idx] > 0). If yes: absorption confirmed (feature is present but information is lost to reconstruction).
absorption_measurement_algorithm.step_8: Absorption rate = count(step 7 confirmed) / count(step 2 correct).
absorption_measurement_algorithm.strict_vs_compensatory: Strict absorption: main feature does NOT fire. Compensatory absorption: main feature fires but probe still fails (information absorbed into other latents).

methodology_notes.title: Activation Patching Methodology Correction: Pilot Bug and FULL-Mode Fix
methodology_notes.description: Critical methodology bug in pilot corrected in FULL mode.

pilot_bug.description: Pilot zeroed features with highest POSITIVE cosine to probe.
pilot_bug.root_cause: Misidentification: positive cosine = support features, not absorbers.

full_mode_fix.description: FULL mode: contribution = activation * cosine. Negative contribution = absorbers.

details.lesson: Absorber identification requires CONTRIBUTION analysis (activation * cosine), not just cosine similarity.
details.paper_impact: Section 5.2 must use d=1.50 (city-continent), d=0.75 (city-language), not d=-0.91.

methodology_notes.title: Decoder Direction Magnitude: Cross-Hierarchy Comparison
methodology_notes.description: Decoder direction magnitude analysis extended from city-continent to first-letter.

method.step_1: Identify absorption instances (probe correct on raw, incorrect on SAE).
method.step_2: Ablate parent direction from child decoder: d_mod = d_child - proj(d_child, d_parent).
method.step_3: Measure logit change for correct class with modified decoder.
method.step_4: Control: ablate random direction of same norm.

details.circularity: Measures decoder geometry, not computational redundancy.

key_messages: Full reproducibility: seed=42, versions, SAE IDs.
